sections.paragraph:
fut. יִנְעַם to be pleasant, lovely, used of one beloved, Son 7:7 a friend, 2Sa 1:26 to be pleasant, used of a country, Gen 49:15 impers. Pro 24:25, לַמֹּוכִיחִים יִנְעָֽם “to those who punish (i.e. judges), there shall be delight,” i.e. it shall be well with them; comp. יִיטַב לִי, טֹוב לִי it is well with me. (Arab. نَعِمَ and نَعُمَ i.q. Hebr., نَعَمَ to delight in any thing.)

Derivatives, the seven following nouns, and נָעִים, מַנְעַמִּים.
